name: Mondays
description: The first day of the week — songs, sayings, traditions, and the case for hating it.

questions.q: What does the name 'Monday' literally mean in Old English?

source.notes: From Old English 'Mōnandæg.'

questions.q: What is the Latin name for Monday — 'dies' of which celestial body?

source.notes: Dies Lunae = 'Day of the Moon.' Survives in French 'lundi,' Spanish 'lunes,' Italian 'lunedì.'

questions.q: In Portuguese, Monday is called 'segunda-feira.' What does it literally mean?

source.notes: 'Feira' was originally a market day; the system uses ordinal numbers Mon-Fri.

questions.q: By the ISO 8601 international standard, which day is the first day of the week?

source.notes: Most of the world treats Monday as the first day of the week; the U.S./Canada often start with Sunday.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, which deity is most associated with Monday?

source.notes: Many Hindus fast on Mondays in honor of Shiva.

questions.q: Which 1966 hit by The Mamas & the Papas mentions Monday in its title and chorus?
choices: Dedicated to the One I Love

source.notes: Reached #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 1966.

questions.q: Which 1986 Bangles hit was written by Prince?

source.notes: Reached #2 on the Hot 100 — kept off #1 by Prince's own 'Kiss.'

questions.q: Which Carpenters song begins, 'Talkin' to myself and feelin' old'?

source.notes: 1971 single by Karen and Richard Carpenter.

questions.q: Which 1979 Boomtown Rats song became Bob Geldof's first #1?

source.notes: Inspired by the Cleveland school shooter Brenda Spencer's chilling explanation.

questions.q: Which orange cartoon cat famously hates Mondays?

source.notes: Created by Jim Davis in 1978.

questions.q: What is the name of the post-Thanksgiving Monday for online shopping?

source.notes: Coined in 2005 by Shop.org.

questions.q: What is 'Black Monday' in stock-market history?

source.notes: Dow Jones fell 22.6% in a single session — its largest one-day percentage loss ever.

questions.q: In Christian tradition, what is the Monday after Easter Sunday called?

source.notes: 'Wet Monday' is the Polish nickname (Śmigus-Dyngus).

questions.q: In the Eastern Orthodox calendar, what is the Monday that begins Great Lent called?

source.notes: Καθαρά Δευτέρα — the first day of Great Lent.

questions.q: Which English Manchester rock band, active 1980-1993, was named after the day of the week?

source.notes: Madchester / dance-rock band fronted by Shaun Ryder.

questions.q: On which Monday in 1929 did the U.S. stock market crash, ending the Roaring Twenties?

source.notes: Followed by Black Tuesday (Oct 29) — the actual crash. Both named in retrospect.

questions.q: What does the German word for Monday — 'Montag' — literally mean?

source.notes: Mond = Moon.

questions.q: Which day of the week was Muhammad born and received his first revelation, according to Islamic tradition?

source.notes: Many Muslims fast voluntarily on Mondays for this reason.

questions.q: Which 'Office' character once said 'I declared bankruptcy!' but is also famous for hating Mondays?

source.notes: Garfield's anti-Monday tirades are a comic-strip staple.

questions.q: In Spain, what unlucky day-of-week superstition replaces 'Friday the 13th'?

source.notes: 'Martes 13' — Tuesday is associated with Mars (god of war). Common in Greek tradition too.
